note: seq=11 (queue): A.T.L.A.S. METRICS EXPORTER -- the Hetzner half of the cockpit bridge. Computes the V3 dashboard KPIs READ-ONLY from the live Postgres (tuanichat_atlas / schema atlas): count(atlas.business), enrich_queue depth by status (pending/claimed/done/failed/dead), per-source counts from atlas.source_record, intake rate/min over a trailing window, and last_updated. PUBLISHES them as status/<node>/atlas-metrics.json via the EXISTING status-back channel (GitHub Contents API, STATUS_TOKEN/STATUS_REPO from /etc/atlas/autopull.env) -- the SAME proven push model atlas_enrich_worker.py already uses. SECURITY: does NOT open any new port and does NOT expose Postgres publicly; the box pushes a tiny JSON up, WordPress only reads the raw file over HTTPS. DB creds come from /etc/atlas/db.env (PGHOST/PGDATABASE/PGUSER/PGPASSWORD). Connection is SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY -> SELECT-only -> rollback; no DML/DDL ever. Schema is INTROSPECTED from information_schema (not hardcoded) so it matches whatever the real atlas column names are; any missing column degrades that KPI to null (honest), never fakes a number. FAIL-SOFT: DB-down or GitHub-unreachable still emits an honest reachable=false envelope and exits 0 so the timer never wedges and the dashboard shows 'ATLAS: connecting...'. Installs a RECURRING systemd timer (every 5 min, offset from other timers) AND runs once now. Fetch+sha-verify atlas_metrics_export.py from scripts/; pip installs psycopg2-binary. HONEST: the live DB read + the GitHub PUT were NOT exercised on the build host (sandbox has no Postgres and no outbound network); --selftest degrades cleanly (exit 3) with no Postgres and the py_compile + dry-run fail-soft path are verified. The --selftest step in this manifest is the gate: it connects, verifies atlas.business exists, computes the metrics, rolls back, and the install is auto-rolled-back if it fails. Published as seq=11 (real next-free after EDGAR seq-10).

steps.note: seq=11: install atlas-metrics timer (recurring, 5min, offset) + fetch/verify/run the read-only KPI exporter once now. Read-only on the data; pushes status/<node>/atlas-metrics.json via the existing status-back token.

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS read-only metrics exporter for the Jarvis V3 cockpit (count atlas.business + enrich_queue depth + per-source + intake -> status/<node>/atlas-metrics.json via status-back)
Documentation=https://github.com/cloudwalker171/atlas-manifests
After=network-online.target postgresql.service
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
Nice=10
Environment=ATLAS_DB_ENV=/etc/atlas/db.env
Environment=ATLAS_AUTOPULL_CONF=/etc/atlas/autopull.env
ExecStart=/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python /opt/atlas/importers/atlas_metrics_export.py
TimeoutStartSec=5min

[Unit]
Description=Publish ATLAS cockpit metrics every 5 min (read-only KPIs -> status-back JSON the WordPress dashboard reads)

[Timer]
OnBootSec=4min
OnUnitActiveSec=5min
Persistent=true
Unit=atlas-metrics.service

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/importers/atlas_metrics_export.py':'4df9e2be1645091ec9339e1f84b89eebcf46f9a181e99669f8a908429af0ddda'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"
